title: Publicly-posted output is a leak surface: CI guard renders public-only by construction
context: The leakage review swarm (21 agents, 16/17 confirmed) found the private-overlay's leak-safety guarantee (dec_d7bad4ccb7) only covered COMMITTED files. But the CI Constraint Guard runs `hunch check --format markdown` and posts it as a PUBLIC PR comment, and buildCheckReport is fully recs-backed (checkConstraints/vetoHits/regressionHits/causalChain) — so a private constraint matching a changed file WOULD render into a public PR comment if HUNCH_PRIVATE_DIR were ever set in CI. Safe only by convention (env unset in CI), not by construction.
decision: Extend the leak-safety invariant: a PUBLICLY-POSTED output (PR comment, shared CI log) is a leak surface equal to a committed file. Add a publicOnly mode to buildCheckReport (a transient suppressPrivate flag gating recs() to public-only) and a `hunch check --public-only` flag. The scaffolded CI guard (ciAction.ts) now ALWAYS passes --public-only AND sets env HUNCH_PRIVATE_DIR="" on the guard step (defense-in-depth) — so the public PR comment is public-only BY CONSTRUCTION regardless of env. LOCAL `hunch check` and the pre-edit hook stay merged, so private constraints are still ENFORCED locally. Also: gitignore scaffolder excludes .hunch-private/; hunch_record_correction gained private routing (a sensitive rule enforced locally, never posted); private writes skip the public `existing` lookup so a private record never inherits public fields.
consequences: A private constraint is enforced on the developer's machine (pre-edit hook + local check) but can never appear in the CI PR comment — the two surfaces are intentionally asymmetric.
merge_verdict (MCP) stays merged: its output is returned to the local assistant, not auto-posted; the user owns whether to paste it publicly (documented).
Locked in by a regression test: renderMarkdown over a report built with publicOnly contains none of a private constraint's text, while the merged report does.
alternatives_rejected: Tag each rendered record with private-origin and redact in markdown — more code + a redaction-miss risk; a clean public-only read at the source is simpler and provably complete.
Make markdown format imply public-only — would surprise local users who want private memory in a local markdown report; an explicit --public-only flag (always set by the CI scaffold) is clearer.
